# Real-World Scenarios

##  Interview: You're asked to explain a caching system design. What happens if you've always relied on AI to design systems?

    Ans: If I've always relied on AI without proper knowledge about the caching system design since it wasn't completely my brain-child but a product of AI, it will be difficult - if not impossible to give a candid explanation because it's not my work but technically a stolen property. 

    
## Production Bug: At 2 AM, you must debug code generated by AI months ago - but AI is offline. Can you fix it?

    Ans: No, I can not fix it but i will try because, it is a generated code which i have little or no knowledge about it's scope and logic. Even if i did before now, I must have forgotten because I did not coin it out of my brain on my own but just forged ahead with production of a code that i can not defend and now that there's a bug i have no idea on where to rectify it from, without AI.

## New Technology: A new library is released, and AI hasn't been trained on it yet. How do you learn it?

    Ans: I will learn it by going through their official documentation on the web, engage in practical learning by watching videos and getting myself equipped with hands-on approach to understand the library and it's technicalities.

## Reflection: Write one paragraph explaining how using AI fairly today prepares you for real-world challenges like these.

    Ans: 
        Using AI fairly today prepares me for real-world challenges like these by stimulating my brain to think, properly using it as a tool to learn and provide actual solutions to the problems at hand from my understanding of the language I'm using to code and aiding me to properly finetune the code to a better version which covers all aspects that might cause issues later on.
